summary: Senior Frontend Engineer with 12+ years building production web products across B2B SaaS, enterprise platforms, marketplaces, and Web3 security. Deep in TypeScript, React, CSS, React Router, Storybook, and developer-facing tooling, with recent work spanning OpenZeppelin's Secure Audit frontend, SDK capabilities for external developers, and a TypeScript monorepo refactor that cut build times from about 15 minutes to 3 minutes. At Premise and Resit, integrated Storybook to improve how designers and developers worked together, with a focus on shared components, clearer handoff, and UI consistency. Brings production ownership across CI, monitoring, payment integrations, and AI-assisted development.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Co-led OpenZeppelin Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers, building a TypeScript and React frontend with Zustand so auditors could work with clients, track findings, verify fixes, and deliver final PDF reports.
Built SDK capabilities for Defender and the open-source Relayer so developers and technical teams could integrate the products and build custom automation tooling.

Refactored the TypeScript monorepo build system through ESM migration, package exports, bundler and tsconfig updates, lint, and typecheck flow, cutting build times from about 15 minutes to 3 minutes while reducing bundle size.
Led Playwright end-to-end testing for critical Defender user flows, improving release confidence for a complex production frontend.
Owned production-facing Defender work, including Stripe billing integration for Pro and enterprise plans and alert tuning that cut Slack and PagerDuty noise by about 60%.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Built a React and TypeScript presentation feature in MyPremise that surfaced up-to-date, often live data through one shareable URL, replacing repeated PDF or PPT exports after each update.
Integrated Storybook with Material UI to improve designer and developer handoff, document shared components, reduce prototyping time from one week to one day, and cut UI bugs.
Contributed to the migration from REST to GraphQL with Apollo across key entities, including Express.js service updates, improving API response times by 35% and lowering server usage.
Expanded Jest coverage and added GitHub Actions quality gates, reducing release cycles from one week to less than 24 hours by cutting manual testing effort.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Led development of an internal integration and onboarding platform with Go, Node.js, and Angular, removing dependency on external engineering support, saving $75,000 annually, and helping customer service teams onboard merchants faster.
Built merchant ingestion pipelines that normalized data from FTP, APIs, SOAP XML feeds, and web scraping for pricing, stock, availability, bundles, and product attributes.

Implemented real-time catalog and pricing updates with Go and Kafka, moving core logic from Python ETL so product and discount changes reached the app faster and contributed to a 21% increase in CSAT.

workExperience.title: System Architect
description: Improved architecture and delivery for a legacy internal platform, working with product, engineering, and infrastructure teams to reduce delivery cycles from months to about two weeks.
Implemented transaction-related API flows with Express.js, including Transbank payment processing integrations.
Introduced DevOps practices and led a staged migration from on-prem to Azure hybrid infrastructure across applications, databases, jobs, storage, and networking.

workExperience.title: Full Stack Developer
description: Delivered more than five web and mobile products for clients across medical, industrial, government, legal, and cultural sectors, working from discovery and requirements through planning and implementation.
Integrated Storybook into React and TypeScript workflows to improve collaboration between designers and developers and make shared UI work easier to review and reuse.
Led architecture decisions for each project and set engineering standards for TypeScript, React, and React Native across the company.
Mentored five developers through pairing sessions, code reviews, and workshops focused on architecture, testing, and code quality.

workExperience.title: Fullstack Developer
description: Contributed to building Dxpress from idea to launch as one of the first Uber Eats-style delivery services in Chile, working with a small product and engineering team.
Applied Angular on operational interfaces and built Python and Go services for search, geolocation, real-time operations, and ETL workflows that normalized catalogs with more than 5,000 products.

workExperience.title: Junior Software Developer
description: Started as a junior developer and took on growing frontend ownership, helping introduce Node.js and AngularJS into the agency stack.

education.degree: Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science

company_profile.description: Paddle is a Merchant of Record platform for digital product businesses, managing payments, tax, compliance, fraud, billing, checkout, subscriptions, invoicing, reporting, and retention so software sellers can operate and grow globally with less operational complexity.
company_profile.mission: Help digital product companies operate and grow automatically by transforming complexity into simplicity.
company_profile.vision: Reach every market and capture every opportunity by helping sellers scale globally without adding payment, tax, compliance, and billing complexity.
products: Official Node.js, PHP, Go, and Python SDKs
services: Merchant of Record services for digital products
Developer APIs, SDKs, webhooks, and integration tooling
company_profile.industry: Fintech / Merchant of Record / SaaS billing infrastructure
company_profile.business_model: Merchant of Record billing platform for digital product companies, with managed payments, tax, compliance, fraud, checkout, subscriptions, analytics, and retention tooling.
recent_signals: Paddle says it manages payments, tax, compliance, and billing across 300+ markets as Merchant of Record for digital product businesses.
Paddle's homepage reports 190 million transactions processed, $130 million in sales taxes remitted last year, and 10,000+ customers using Paddle.
The provided JD says Paddle serves over 6,000 software sellers in 245 territories globally.
Paddle's developer docs describe Paddle Billing as an all-in-one billing platform and document Paddle API, SDKs, webhooks, and Paddle.js.
Paddle publishes official SDKs for Node.js, PHP, Go, and Python, plus a Paddle.js wrapper and a Next.js starter kit.
Paddle developer docs advertise a docs MCP server and AI/IDE integration path for up-to-date Paddle knowledge.
Paddle is promoting Lovable Payments, powered by Paddle, with the official tagline 'From prompt to payments.'

source_notes.claim: Paddle homepage and official navigation were checked for product, developer, resources, and careers links.
Paddle is a Merchant of Record for digital product businesses and manages payments, tax, compliance, and billing across 300+ markets.
Paddle careers page says it helps digital product companies operate and grow automatically and transforms complexity into simplicity.
Paddle values are Paddle for others, Paddle together, and Paddle simply.
Paddle describes its work model as digital first with hubs, trust, and autonomy.
Careers page was checked and visible open roles were captured from https://www.paddle.com/careers; target Senior Frontend Engineer was found with location UK, Portugal, Ireland.

Target role URL from official careers list is https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/paddle/18a95452-e913-4d55-af72-8700eb640b93; webfetch returned only a JavaScript-required shell, so user-provided JD remains the full text source.
Paddle developer docs cover Paddle API, SDKs, webhooks, Paddle.js, official SDKs, a Paddle.js wrapper, a Next.js starter kit, and AI/MCP documentation tools.

Provided JD says the Experience Group includes Dashboard and Developer Experience teams, covering dashboard products, the design system, developer.paddle.com, SDKs, public libraries, and integration tooling.
